TEXT.citation: Tamih and Montgomery, Robert L. (1962) Amis text. Work Papers of the Summer Institute of Linguistics, University of North Dakota Session: Vol. 6, Article 6. 10.31356/silwp.vol06.06
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{montgomery1962amis, title={Amis text}, author={Tamih and Montgomery, Robert L}, journal={Work Papers of the Summer Institute of Linguistics, University of North Dakota Session}, volume={6}, number={6}, doi={10.31356/silwp.vol06.06}}

S.FORM: O-LEMAK AKO ANINI
S.PHON: o~u-dəmak ako~u anini
S.FORM: OLEMAK AKO ANINI
S.PHON: o~uɾəmak ako~u anini

S.FORM: pito ko toki to lafak romoal kako nay-foti?
S.PHON: pito~u ko~u to~uki to~u dafak ɾo~umo~uad kako~u naj-fo~utiʡ
S.FORM: pito ko toki to dafak lomoad kako nayfoti'
S.PHON: pito~u ko~u to~uki to~u ɬ~ɮafak ɾo~umo~uaɬ~ɮ kako~u najfo~utiʡ
S.TRANSL: At seven in the morning I get up from sleep.

S.FORM: na-romoal tangsor miso?ats to walis marari?op kako
S.PHON: na-ɾo~umo~uad taŋso~uɾ miso~uʡaʦ to~u wadis maɾaɾiʡo~up kako~u
S.FORM: nalomoad tangsol miso'ac to wadis malali'op kako
S.PHON: naɾo~umo~uaɬ~ɮ taŋso~uɾ miso~uʡaʦ to~u waɬ~ɮis maɾaɾiʡo~up kako~u
S.TRANSL: Having gotten up, right away I brush my teeth and wash my face.

S.FORM: ma-hřek a ma-rari?op ?i mi-sa?osi kako to fangtsar-ay a tsolal
S.PHON: ma-ħɾək a ma-ɾaɾiʡo~up ʡi mi-saʡo~usi kako~u to~u faŋʦaɾ-aj a ʦo~udad
S.FORM: mahrek a malali'op 'i misa'osi kako to fangcalay a codad
S.PHON: maħrək a maɾaɾiʡo~up ʡi misaʡo~usi kako~u to~u faŋʦaɾaj a ʦo~uɬ~ɮaɬ~ɮ
S.TRANSL: When I finish washing my face, I read the Bible.

S.FORM: pangkiw saka-faro no toki ma-řanam kami
S.PHON: paŋkiw saka-faɾo~u no~u to~uki ma-ɾanam kami
S.FORM: pangkiw sakafalo no toki maranam kami
S.PHON: paŋkiw sakafaɾo~u no~u to~uki maranam kami
S.TRANSL: At half past eight o'clock, we eat breakfast.

S.FORM: anini a řomi?al ?i tayřa kako i posko
S.PHON: anini a ɾo~umiʡad ʡi tajɾa kako~u i po~usko~u
S.FORM: anini a romi'ad 'i tayra kako i posko
S.PHON: anini a ro~umiʡaɬ~ɮ ʡi tajra kako~u i po~usko~u
S.TRANSL: Today I am going to Posko.

S.FORM: iřa ko sa?opo no tsiwro ato sitsotsi itiřa mara-pa-pasifana?-ay kako
S.PHON: iɾa ko~u saʡo~upo~u no~u ʦiwɾo~u ato~u siʦo~uʦi itiɾa maɾa-pa-pasifanaʡ-aj kako~u
S.FORM: ira ko sa'opo no ciwlo ato sicoci itira malapapasifana'ay kako
S.PHON: ira ko~u saʡo~upo~u no~u ʦiwɾo~u ato~u siʦo~uʦi itira maɾapapasifanaʡaj kako~u
S.TRANSL: There is a meeting of elders and deacons there whom I will teach.

S.FORM: mahřek a mařanam mara-rosil to kako to saka-tayřa i posko
S.PHON: maħɾək a maɾanam maɾa-ɾo~usid to~u kako~u to~u saka-tajɾa i po~usko~u
S.FORM: mahrek a maranam malalosid to kako to sakatayra i posko
S.PHON: maħrək a maranam maɾaɾo~usiɬ~ɮ to~u kako~u to~u sakatajra i po~usko~u
S.TRANSL: When breakfast is finished I prepare to go to Posko.

S.FORM: mimaan kiso mama saan ko wawa ako a mi-ritsay
S.PHON: mimaan kiso~u mama saan ko~u wawa ako~u a mi-ɾiʦaj
S.FORM: mimaan kiso mama saan ko wawa ako a milicay
S.PHON: mimaan kiso~u mama saan ko~u wawa ako~u a miɾiʦaj
S.TRANSL: "What are you doing father," so asks my child.

S.FORM: tayřa kako i posko han ako
S.PHON: tajɾa kako~u i po~usko~u ħan ako~u
S.FORM: tayra kako i posko han ako
S.PHON: tajra kako~u i po~usko~u ħan ako~u

S.FORM: ka-itini ito i roma? pi-tengir to soar ni ina haw han ako tsinřa
S.PHON: ka-itini ito~u i ɾo~umaʡ pi-təŋiɾ to~u so~uaɾ ni ina ħaw ħan ako~u ʦinɾa
S.FORM: kaitini ito i loma' pitengil to soal ni ina haw han ako cinra
S.PHON: kaitini ito~u i ɾo~umaʡ pitəŋiɾ to~u so~uaɾ ni ina ħaw ħan ako~u ʦinra
S.TRANSL: "Stay here at home; obey your mother's words, O.K.?" I say to him.

S.FORM: siwa ko toki mi-lakaw to kako to kasoring pa-sayřa i posko
S.PHON: siwa ko~u to~uki mi-dakaw to~u kako~u to~u kaso~uɾiŋ pa-sajɾa i po~usko~u
S.FORM: siwa ko toki midakaw to kako to kasoling pasayra i posko
S.PHON: siwa ko~u to~uki miɬ~ɮakaw to~u kako~u to~u kaso~uɾiŋ pasajra i po~usko~u
S.TRANSL: At nine o'clock I get on the gasoline train to go to Posko.

S.FORM: tahiřa kako i posko tstsay ko safaw no toki
S.PHON: taħiɾa kako~u i po~usko~u ʦʦaj ko~u safaw no~u to~uki
S.FORM: tahira kako i posko ccay ko safaw no toki
S.PHON: taħira kako~u i po~usko~u ʦʦaj ko~u safaw no~u to~uki
S.TRANSL: I arrive in Posko at eleven (?) o'clock.

S.FORM: tahiřa han ako ma-sa?opo ito ko ?aromanay a tsiwro ato sitsotsi
S.PHON: taħiɾa ħan ako~u ma-saʡo~upo~u ito~u ko~u ʡaɾo~umanaj a ʦiwɾo~u ato~u siʦo~uʦi
S.FORM: tahira han ako masa'opo ito ko 'alomanay a ciwlo ato sicoci
S.PHON: taħira ħan ako~u masaʡo~upo~u ito~u ko~u ʡaɾo~umanaj a ʦiwɾo~u ato~u siʦo~uʦi
S.TRANSL: When I arrived there gathered many elders and deacons.

S.FORM: ripahak ko farotso? ako a ma-sasi?af̆a?ařaw to ?ařomanay a tamlaw
S.PHON: ɾipaħak ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ ako~u a ma-sasiʡaf*aʡaɾaw to~u ʡaɾo~umanaj a tamdaw
S.FORM: lipahak ko faloco' ako a masasi'af̆a'araw to 'aromanay a tamdaw
S.PHON: ɾipaħak ko~u faɾo~uʦo~uʡ ako~u a masasiʡaf*aʡaraw to~u ʡaro~umanaj a tamɬ~ɮaw
S.TRANSL: My heart was happy because so many people came together.

S.FORM: spot ko toki to hřek no rohok mi-lakaw htsa kako to kasoring a mi-nokay
S.PHON: spo~ut ko~u to~uki to~u ħɾək no~u ɾo~uħo~uk mi-dakaw ħʦa kako~u to~u kaso~uɾiŋ a mi-no~ukaj
S.FORM: spot ko toki to hrek no lohok midakaw hca kako to kasoling a minokay
S.PHON: spo~ut ko~u to~uki to~u ħrək no~u ɾo~uħo~uk miɬ~ɮakaw ħʦa kako~u to~u kaso~uɾiŋ a mino~ukaj
S.TRANSL: At four o'clock in the afternoon I get on once more the gasoline train and return.

S.FORM: tahiřa han ako i roma? ma-tahkar ito ko saka-rafi
S.PHON: taħiɾa ħan ako~u i ɾo~umaʡ ma-taħkaɾ ito~u ko~u saka-ɾafi
S.FORM: tahira han ako i loma' matahkal ito ko sakalafi
S.PHON: taħira ħan ako~u i ɾo~umaʡ mataħkaɾ ito~u ko~u sakaɾafi
S.TRANSL: When I arrived home food is already prepared for supper.

S.FORM: ripahak ko forotso? ako anini nawhani fangtsar ko řakot ako i fatal no raran
S.PHON: ɾipaħak ko~u fo~uɾo~uʦo~uʡ ako~u anini nawħani faŋʦaɾ ko~u ɾako~ut ako~u i fatad no~u ɾaɾan
S.FORM: lipahak ko foloco' ako anini nawhani fangcal ko rakot ako i fatad no lalan
S.PHON: ɾipaħak ko~u fo~uɾo~uʦo~uʡ ako~u anini nawħani faŋʦaɾ ko~u rako~ut ako~u i fataɬ~ɮ no~u ɾaɾan
S.TRANSL: My heart is happy now because I had a fine trip on the way.
